Joseph tried to see how his brothers felt about Benjamin. If they envied and hated the other son of Rachel as they had hated him, and if they had the same lack of feeling towards their father Jacob as before, they would now show it. When the cup was found with Benjamin, they would have a reason to leave him to be a slave. But we cannot judge what people are now by what they have been in the past, nor what they will do by what they have done. The steward accused them of being ungrateful, rewarding evil for good; of being foolish, in taking away the cup that was used every day, which would soon be missed, and a careful search made for it; for so it is written: Is this not the one with which my lord drinks, as having a particular fondness for it, and for which he would search thoroughly? Or, by which, leaving it carelessly at your table, he would test whether you are honest men or not? They put themselves in Joseph’s mercy, and acknowledge God's righteousness, perhaps thinking of the injury they had formerly done to Joseph, for which they thought God was now holding them accountable. Even in afflictions where we believe ourselves wronged by people, we must admit that God is righteous and reveals our sin.

If Joseph had been, as Judah supposed him, a complete stranger to the family, he could not help but be moved by his powerful arguments. But neither Jacob nor Benjamin needed someone to plead with Joseph; for he loved them himself. Judah’s faithful support of Benjamin, now in his distress, was rewarded long afterwards by the tribe of Benjamin remaining with the tribe of Judah, when the other tribes deserted it. The apostle, when speaking about Christ's mediation, observes that our Lord came from the tribe of Judah; and he not only made intercession for those who sin, but he became a Surety for them, showing tender concern both for his Father and for his brothers. Jesus, the great example of Joseph, humbles and tests his people even after they have experienced some of his kindness. He brings their sins to their remembrance so that they may practice and show repentance, and feel how much they owe to his mercy.
